# Automated Assembly Line A1 Emergency Response Plan

**Document Number**: EP-2024-001
**Version**: v2.0
**Preparation Date**: January 2024
**Scope of Application**: Automated Assembly Line A1 and surrounding areas

---

## 1. General Provisions

### 1.1 Purpose

This plan is formulated to effectively prevent, timely control and eliminate hazards from emergencies of Automated Assembly Line A1, minimize casualties and property losses caused by emergencies, and ensure equipment safety and smooth production.

### 1.2 Scope of Application

This plan applies to emergency response for various emergencies occurring in Automated Assembly Line A1, including fire, leakage, power outage, equipment failure, personnel injury and other accidents.

---

## 2. Emergency Response Organization

### 2.1 Emergency Command Center

| Role | Name | Responsibility |
|------|------|----------------|
| Commander-in-Chief | Manager Wang | Fully responsible for emergency response |
| Deputy Commander | Supervisor Li | Assist commander, responsible for on-site command |
| Team Member | Zhang Maintenance | Equipment emergency response |
| Team Member | Security Personnel | On-site security and evacuation |

### 2.2 Emergency Response Departments

| Department | Responsibility |
|------------|----------------|
| Security Department | On-site security, personnel evacuation, safety guard |
| Equipment Department | Equipment emergency response, fault troubleshooting |
| First Aid Department | On-site first aid, medical rescue |
| Logistics Department | Material support, communication |

---

## 3. Emergency Response Procedures

### 3.1 Fire Emergency Response

**Trigger Condition**: Fire detected or fire alarm

**Response Procedure**:

| Step | Response Action | Department | Time Limit | Success Criterion----------------|------------|------------| |
|------|------------------|
| 1 | Immediately press alarm button upon discovering fire | On-duty staff | Immediately | Alarm signal sent |
| 2 | Call internal alarm phone to report fire | On-duty staff | Within 1 min | Report successful |
| 3 | Activate fire broadcast to notify personnel evacuation | Security Department | Within 2 min | Broadcast covers entire area |
| 4 | Open fire hydrants and use fire extinguishers for initial firefighting | Equipment Department | Within 3 min | Fire controlled |
| 5 | Turn off power and gas valves in fire area | Equipment Department | Within 2 min | Power cut off |
| 6 | Evacuate production area personnel to safe assembly point | Security Department | Within 5 min | All personnel evacuated |
| 7 | Call 119 to request fire brigade support | Security Department | Immediately | Alarm successful |
| 8 | Welcome fire brigade and provide on-site information | Security Department | Within 10 min | Fire brigade arrived |

### 3.2 Chemical Leakage Emergency Response

**Trigger Condition**: Chemical leakage detected

**Response Procedure**:

| Step | Response Action | Department | Time Limit | Success Criterion |
|------|----------------|------------|------------|------------------|
| 1 | Stop operation immediately, close leakage source | On-duty staff | Immediately | Leakage source closed |
| 2 | Activate ventilation equipment to dilute leakage concentration | Equipment Department | Within 2 min | Ventilation activated |
| 3 | Use adsorption materials to contain leakage | Equipment Department | Within 3 min | Leakage contained |
| 4 | Evacuate surrounding personnel, set up警戒区域 | Security Department | Within 5 min | Personnel evacuated |
| 5 | Call emergency phone to request medical support | Security Department | Immediately | Medical support requested |
| 6 | Clean up leakage, resume production | Equipment Department | Within 30 min | Site cleaned up |

### 3.3 Power Outage Emergency Response

**Trigger Condition**: Sudden power outage

**Response Procedure**:

| Step | Response Action | Department | Time Limit | Success Criterion |
|------|----------------|------------|------------|------------------|
| 1 | Immediately start backup power | Equipment Department | Within 10 sec | Backup power started |
| 2 | Activate emergency lighting to ensure personnel safety evacuation | Equipment Department | Within 30 sec | Emergency lighting activated |
| 3 | Shut down main equipment to prevent damage | Equipment Department | Within 1 min | Equipment shut down |
| 4 | Personnel evacuate production area in order | Security Department | Within 3 min | Personnel evacuated |
| 5 | Investigate cause of power outage, assess recovery time | Equipment Department | Within 10 min | Cause identified |
| 6 | After power restored, restart equipment in sequence | Equipment Department | Within 20 min | Equipment restarted |

### 3.4 Equipment Failure Emergency Response

**Trigger Condition**: Critical equipment serious failure

**Response Procedure**:

| Step | Response Action | Department | Time Limit | Success Criterion |
|------|----------------|------------|------------|------------------|
| 1 | Press emergency stop button, stop equipment operation | Operator | Immediately | Equipment stopped |
| 2 | Cut off equipment power to prevent failure expansion | Operator | Immediately | Power cut off |
| 3 | Report equipment failure situation | Operator | Within 1 min | Report sent |
| 4 | Maintenance personnel arrive for fault diagnosis | Equipment Department | Within 15 min | Diagnosis completed |
| 5 | Assess failure degree, formulate repair plan | Equipment Department | Within 30 min | Plan formulated |
| 6 | Implement repair, resume production | Equipment Department | Within 2 hours | Production resumed |

### 3.5 Personnel Injury Emergency Response

**Trigger Condition**: Personnel injury accident occurs

**Response Procedure**:

| Step | Response Action | Department | Time Limit | Success Criterion |
|------|----------------|------------|------------|------------------|
| 1 | Stop operation immediately, ensure site safety | On-duty staff | Immediately | Site secured |
| 2 | Check injured person's condition | On-duty staff | Immediately | Condition assessed |
| 3 | Perform on-site first aid | First Aid Department | Within 5 min | First aid completed |
| 4 | Call 120 emergency phone | First Aid Department | Immediately | Emergency call made |
| 5 | Arrange personnel to welcome ambulance | First Aid Department | Within 10 min | Ambulance received |
| 6 | Protect site, cooperate with investigation | Security Department | Ongoing | Site protected |

---

## 4. Emergency Support

### 4.1 Material Support

- Fire fighting equipment: Fire extinguishers, fire hydrants, fire broadcast
- First aid supplies: First aid kits, stretchers, protective clothing
- Emergency lighting: Emergency lights, flashlights
- Communication equipment: Walkie-talkies, mobile phones

### 4.2 Training and Drills

- Conduct emergency drills quarterly
- New employee training includes emergency content
- Organize comprehensive emergency drill annually

---

## 5. Plan Management

### 5.1 Plan Revision

This plan is revised annually, and should be revised promptly if major changes occur.

### 5.2 Plan Interpretation

This plan is interpreted by the Safety and Environmental Protection Department.

---

**Prepared by**: Safety and Environmental Protection Department
**Reviewed by**: Production Department
**Approved by**: General Manager
**Effective Date**: January 15, 2024
